A: Exit? No.
A: ["Roger O. Egeberg", "Douglas J. Bennet", "Office of Population Affairs", "Timeline of the presidency of Gerald Ford"]
A: Roger O. Egeberg was Assistant Secretary for Health and Scientific Affairs during the Nixon administration. The president of the United States that served during that time was Richard Nixon, and the years of his administration were 1969 to 1974. However, the term of service for Assistant Secretary of Health, Education, and Welfare,  was in the years 1969-1970, and again in 1970-1971 (during the Nixon administration), but after 1 year of not serving, in 1973 -1977 during the Nixon administration and the Ford administration.

A: ["Roger O. Egeberg", "Douglas J. Bennet", "Office of Population Affairs", "Timeline of the presidency of Gerald Ford", "Second inauguration of Richard Nixon"]
A: Roger O. Egeberg was Assistant Secretary for Health and Scientific Affairs during the Nixon administration. The president of the United States that served during that time was Richard Nixon, and the years of his administration were 1969 to 1974. However, the term of service for Assistant Secretary of Health, Education, and Welfare,  was in the years 1969-1970, and again in 1970-1971 (during the Nixon administration), but after 1 year of not serving, in 1973 -1977 during the Nixon administration and the Ford administration.   So the answer is: 1969-1977.

A: ["Socialist Revolutionary Party", "Kerensky\u2013Krasnov uprising"]
A: The Socialist Revolutionary Party had a civil war against the Bolsheviks that ended with the defeat and destruction of the SRs when Alexander Kerensky's Provisional Government was overthrown and in January 1918 the Constituent Assembly was disbanded.
A: Exit? No.
A: ["Socialist Revolutionary Party", "Kerensky\u2013Krasnov uprising", "Communism in Russia"]
A: The Socialist Revolutionary Party had a civil war against the Bolsheviks that ended with the defeat and destruction of the SRs when Alexander Kerensky's Provisional Government was overthrown and in January 1918 the Constituent Assembly was disbanded. So the answer is: in January 1918 (Communist Party took power of Russia during 1917).

A: The Livesey Hal War Memorial commemorates the fallen of World War I and World War II.

A: ["Livesey Hall War Memorial", "Masni\u00e8res Newfoundland Memorial", "Rainham War Memorial"]
A: The Livesey Hal War Memorial commemorates the fallen of World War I and World War II. World War I had over 37 million casualties, while World War II had over 50 million casualties, making the total of 87 million.
A: Exit? No.
A: ["Livesey Hall War Memorial", "Masni\u00e8res Newfoundland Memorial", "Rainham War Memorial", "World War II casualties"]
A: The Livesey Hal War Memorial commemorates the fallen of World War I and World War II. World War I had over 37 million casualties, while World War II had over 50 million casualties, making the total of 87 million. However World War II was the deadliest military conflict in history in absolute terms of total casualties, where over 60 million people were killed.
A: Exit? No.
A: ["Livesey Hall War Memorial", "Masni\u00e8res Newfoundland Memorial", "Rainham War Memorial", "World War II casualties", "World War I"]

World War I had over 37 million casualties, while World War II had over 50 million casualties, making the total of 87 million. However World War II was the deadliest military conflict in history in absolute terms of total casualties, where over 60 million people were killed. So the answer is: World War II.

Q: In 1991 Euromarché was bought by a chain that operated how any hypermarkets at the end of 2016?
A: "In 1991, Norsk Hydro acquired the 330 outlets of the Danish operation and five years later it bought the Swedish Uno-X chain from Burmah. It was later renamed as Unimagnat and then the European Retail Group. At the end of 2016, YX Energi operated approximately 146 Uno-X unmanned fuel stations in Norway.  It is unclear how many hypermarkets the original group had in 2016 since they have been mainly fuel stations since 199"
A: In 1991, Norsk Hydro acquired the 330 outlets of the Danish operation and five years later it bought the Swedish Uno-X chain from Burmah. It was later renamed as Unimagnat and then the European Retail Group. At the end of 2016, YX Energi operated approximately 146 Uno-X unmanned fuel stations in Norway.  It is unclear how many hypermarkets the original group had in 2016 since they have been mainly fuel stations since 199

Q: Which Australian city founded in 1838 contains a boarding school opened by a Prime Minister of Australia and named after a school in London of the same name.
A: "Adelaide, the capital of South Australia, was founded in 1836. The school in the question is located in the South Australian suburb of Marion, south of Adelaide.  Prime Minister Robert Menzies opened the school in 1961.  Menzies did not found the school and was not the one to name it; he merely performed the opening ceremony.  In fact Robert Menzies was the Prime Minister of Australia from 26 January 1956 to 10 December 196"
A: Adelaide, the capital of South Australia, was founded in 1836. The school in the question is located in the South Australian suburb of Marion, south of Adelaide.  Prime Minister Robert Menzies opened the school in 1961.  Menzies did not found the school and was not the one to name it; he merely performed the opening ceremony.  In fact Robert Menzies was the Prime Minister of Australia from 26 January 1956 to 10 December 196

Q: D1NZ is a series based on what oversteering technique?

The term drifting originates from the slidey nature of the car and is often compared to the act of cruising, because of the increased speed and style one achieves when executing drifting techniques. So the answer is: drifting."

A: ["pid___ea047a2c40ef2165427213f64bb8434d___3ea63a8fc1b925a580ef05982f224140", "pid___22648a777e7dd656675865f566fcccd9___ce265ce63081a8c90cfd86f17a9c7b0c", "pid___b41d11035ec9012c6e6973ce006dc793___65a3ba17363e47d245722613cc574fef"]
Q: Where did the descendants of the group of black Indians associated with the Seminole people settle?
A: "The descendants of free blacks and of escaped slaves (called maroons) who allied with Seminole groups in Spanish Florida, later to be known as the Black Seminoles, lived mostly in distinct bands near the Native American Seminole. Some were held as slaves of particular Seminole leaders; but they had more freedom than did slaves held by whites in the South and by other Native American tribes, including the right to bear arms. So the answer is: Spanish Florida."
A: Spanish Florida

A: Exit? No.
A: ["514th Flight Test Squadron", "370th Flight Test Squadron", "Hill Air Force Base"]

A: This Major Ployer Peter Hill was test-flying the prototype of the B-17 Flying Fortress bomber after whom the base is named.  The base is located in northern Utah, just south of the city of Ogden, and near the towns of Clearfield, Riverdale, Roy, Sunset, and Layton.  The base was named in honor of Major Ployer Peter Hill of the U.S. Army Air Corps.  The squadron 514th Flight Test Squadron (514

Which component of shellite has the formula (ON)CHOH?
A: ["Semi-empirical mass formula", "Structural formula"]

Q: Which component of shellite has the formula (ON)CHOH?

During Siege of Bharatpur, which Commander-in-Chief of Ireland and Commander-in-Chief of India contributed to the storming of the castle?
A: ["Commander-in-Chief, India", "Alexander George Woodford"]
A: Field Marshal Sir Alexander George Woodford was not Commander-in-Chief of Ireland, but he was Commander-in-Chief of India, and then the Governor and Commander-in-Chief of Gibraltar.
A: Exit? No.
A: ["Commander-in-Chief, India", "Alexander George Woodford", "Claude Auchinleck"]
A: Field Marshal Sir Alexander George Woodford was not Commander-in-Chief of Ireland, but he was Commander-in-Chief of India, and then the Governor and Commander-in-Chief of Gibraltar. During his period as Governor and Commander-in-Chief of Gibraltar, Woodford was absent from the Siege of Bharatpur.
A: Exit? No.
A: ["Commander-in-Chief, India", "Alexander George Woodford", "Claude Auchinleck"]
A: Field Marshal Sir Alexander George Woodford was not Commander-in-Chief of Ireland, but he was Commander-in-Chief of India, and then the Governor and Commander-in-Chief of Gibraltar. During his period as Governor and Commander-in-Chief of Gibraltar, Woodford was absent from the Siege of Bharatpur. Field Marshal Sir Claude John Eyre Auchinleck was Commander-in-Chief India until Partition in 1947, and then the Supreme Commander of all British forces in India and Pakistan until late 1948.

A: ["Aonghus M\u00f3r", "Kingdom of the Isles", "Battle of Anglesey Sound", "Kings of Sicily family tree"]
A: The Kingdom of the Isles.   The Kingdom of the Isles comprised the Hebrides, the islands of the Firth of Clyde and the Isle of Man from the 9th to the 13th centuries AD.
A: Exit? No.
A: ["Aonghus M\u00f3r", "Kingdom of the Isles", "Battle of Anglesey Sound", "Kings of Sicily family tree"]
A: The Kingdom of the Isles.   The Kingdom of the Isles comprised the Hebrides, the islands of the Firth of Clyde and the Isle of Man from the 9th to the 13th centuries AD. The islands concerned are sometimes referred to as the Kingdom of Mann and the Isles, although only some of the later rulers claimed that title.   
A: Exit? No.
A: ["Aonghus M\u00f3r", "Kingdom of the Isles", "Battle of Anglesey Sound", "Kings of Sicily family tree"]
A: The Kingdom of the Isles.   The Kingdom of the Isles comprised the Hebrides, the islands of the Firth of Clyde and the Isle of Man from the 9th to the 13th centuries AD. The islands concerned are sometimes referred to as the Kingdom of Mann and the Isles, although only some of the later rulers claimed that title.    At times the rulers were independent of external control, although for much of the period they had overlords in Norway, Ireland, England, Scotland or Orkney.

At times the rulers were independent of external control, although for much of the period they had overlords in Norway, Ireland, England, Scotland or Orkney. A: Algeria qualified for the first time into the round of 16 in the 2014 FIFA World Cup which was held in Brazil. However the question could be referring to the fact that Brazil had previously held the world cup in 1950 which is when the other final host, Germany hosted it before that it was hosted by Italy (1934). Therefore, the best answer is Brazil.

He was a main character in the arcade game, where the hero must rescue a damsel in distress named Pauline from a giant ape named Donkey Kong. The player is referred to"
A: Donkey Kong. Q: Which composer created Night Ferry first performed in 2012 at Symphony Center in Chicago?
A: "The answer to this question is: Anna Clyne. She is the composer who created the orchestral composition in one movement, Night Ferry. The work was first performed on February 9, 2012, at Symphony Center in Chicago. The composer's Chicago Symphony Orchestra was conducted by Riccardo Muti."

5a8ee4315542990e94052ba7
What officially ended the first phase of the military conflict between the British Raj and the Emirate of Afghanistan from 1878 to 1880?
A: ["Second Anglo-Afghan War", "Emirate of Afghanistan"]
A: The First phase of the military conflict between the British Raj and the Emirate of Afghanistan from 1878 to 1880 was officially ended when the Afghans agreed to let the British attain all of their geopolitical objectives from the Treaty of Gandamak.
A: Exit? No.
A: ["Second Anglo-Afghan War", "Emirate of Afghanistan"]
A: The First phase of the military conflict between the British Raj and the Emirate of Afghanistan from 1878 to 1880 was officially ended when the Afghans agreed to let the British attain all of their geopolitical objectives from the Treaty of Gandamak. Most of the British and Indian soldiers withdrew from Afghanistan.
A: Exit? No.
A: ["Second Anglo-Afghan War", "Emirate of Afghanistan", "Subhas Chandra Bose"]
A: The First phase of the military conflict between the British Raj and the Emirate of Afghanistan from 1878 to 1880 was officially ended when the Afghans agreed to let the British attain all of their geopolitical objectives from the Treaty of Gandamak. Most of the British and Indian soldiers withdrew from Afghanistan. So the answer is: Treaty of Gandamak.
A: ["pid___731c74b6cadb165ec3716dd3112ebde6___ee331bd135864910cfb82b175b2ff162", "pid___324eea7b90a38438cfe045705a0d7613___9facfddeedfad8e1cee1f2731d7ba7ff", "pid___0e2104e23707e8edcf7764b885f0d2ce___22ca9de22f15386c3f267188f2525f96"]
Q: What officially ended the first phase of the military conflict between the British Raj and the Emirate of Afghanistan from 1878 to 1880?
A: "The military conflict ended after the British emerged victorious against the Afghan rebels and the Afghans agreed to let the British attain all of their geopolitical objectives from the Treaty of Gandamak. The Afghans were permitted to maintain internal rule and local customs but they had to cede control of the area's foreign relations to the British, who, in turn, guaranteed the area's freedom from foreign military domination as a buffer between the British Raj and the Russian Empire."
A: The military conflict ended after the British emerged victorious against the Afghan rebels and the Afghans agreed to let the British attain all of their geopolitical objectives from the Treaty of Gandamak. The Afghans were permitted to maintain internal rule and local customs but they had to cede control of the area's foreign relations to the British, who, in turn, guaranteed the area's freedom from foreign military domination as a buffer between the British Raj and the Russian Empire

It is situated on the lower reaches of the Yangtze River and the shores of Lake Tai and belongs to the Yangtze River Delta region. Suzhou is a landlocked city that has a major port (Suzhou Harbor). Taizhou, on the other hand, has a coastal area in the district of Gaogang and a city along the Yangtze River with a harbor that is major for the Yangtze River. Taizhou and its surrounding areas are known as a coastal city area of China. Dafeng District, which was formerly a county, is a district of Yancheng, Jiangsu province.
